rationale: The cited paper is used for more than background listing: it is invoked for a specific mechanism in hypothesis development, especially the role of commensurability/diagnosticity in judgment revision (e.g., overall evaluations being less directly comparable to attribute-level challenge information). The citing article also situates itself as extending the contextual judgment-revision approach associated with prior work including Pham and Muthukrishnan (2002), by adding the positive-versus-negative initial evaluation boundary condition. This gives the citation a clear conceptual role in framing and theory-building rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the judgment-revision framework in Pham and Muthukrishnan (2002), reusing its commensurability/diagnosticity logic to derive predictions about when evaluations will change. It extends that line by testing comparative versus noncomparative recovery strategies under negative initial impressions.
